records.ety: [L. communitas: cf. OF. communit. Cf. Commonalty, and see Common.]

defs: Common possession or enjoyment; participation; as, a community of goods.
quotes: The original community of all things.
An unreserved community of thought and feeling.

defs: A body of people having common rights, privileges, or interests, or living in the same place under the same laws and regulations; as, a community of monks. Hence a number of animals living in a common home or with some apparent association of interests.
Society at large; a commonwealth or state; a body politic; the public, or people in general.
quotes: Burdens upon the poorer classes of the community.
notes: ☞ In this sense, the term should be used with the definite article; as, the interests of the community.

quotes: The essential community of nature between organic growth and inorganic growth.
Eyes . . . sick and blunted with community.
